title: We all owe ADV because of production values tangent
display_title: We all owe ADV because of production values tangent
wikitext:
[]: Es ist sinnvoll. Bl Post by Alaska Slim » Mon Aug 15, 2011 3:43 pm Tarnsman wrote: Tiffany Grant's German was simply better than Miyamura's. But it was still horrible.

[]: Post by Tarnsman » Mon Aug 15, 2011 4:01 pm Alaska Slim wrote: where have you been?

[]: Credit where credit is due. Bl Post by Alaska Slim » Mon Aug 15, 2011 4:42 pm Tarnsman wrote: Not watching dubbed anime. Rebuild was the first ever dub I had ever sat down and watched in its entirety.

[]: Re: Credit where credit is due. Bl Post by Tarnsman » Mon Aug 15, 2011 4:47 pm Alaska Slim wrote: And that tells me you haven't been a fan for very long, as when Eva first arrived on the scene, that wasn't an option.

[]: Re: Credit where credit is due- still Bl-Bl Post by Alaska Slim » Mon Aug 15, 2011 10:21 pm Tarnsman wrote: I got into Eva via subbed VHS tapes in like ~1998/99.

[]: Re: Credit where credit is due- still Bl-Bl Post by gatotsu911 » Mon Aug 15, 2011 10:32 pm Alaska Slim wrote: Even so, who made those tapes? ADV. Thus, you still owe them.

[]: Re: Es ist sinnvoll. Bl Post by Bagheera » Mon Aug 15, 2011 10:36 pm Alaska Slim wrote: She got the annunciation right, the only real problem was she didn't have an accent of whatever region of Germany Asuka might be from. She sounded like an American speaking German, which, since Asuka was an American citizen anyway, you could say it actually fit.

[]: Re: Credit where credit is due- still Bl-Bl Post by Alaska Slim » Mon Aug 15, 2011 10:38 pm gatotsu911 wrote: Owe them what, exactly? He bought the tapes, with money. What more does he owe them?

[]: Re: Credit where credit is due- still Bl-Bl Post by Bagheera » Mon Aug 15, 2011 10:43 pm Alaska Slim wrote: Thanks for bringing both versions State-side, in once again, a time where distributors were happy to just throw their dub on something and call it a day. ADV provided a choice, along with extra-content such as the Director's Cut as soon as they got it.

[]: Re: Credit where credit is due- still Bl-Bl Post by Alaska Slim » Mon Aug 15, 2011 10:54 pm Bagheera wrote: You do know that a lot of people who got into Eva around that time did it via fansubs, right?

[]: Re: Credit where credit is due- still Bl-Bl Post by Bagheera » Mon Aug 15, 2011 10:58 pm Alaska Slim wrote: Yes, and having had the chance to watch said fansubs, compared to ADV's versions, they were largely sh*t.

[]: Fan sub... B/ Post by Alaska Slim » Mon Aug 15, 2011 11:20 pm Bagheera wrote: You've moving goalposts like an Olympic champion, dude.

[]: Re: Fan sub... B/ Post by Bagheera » Mon Aug 15, 2011 11:36 pm Alaska Slim wrote: And you're suggesting something more than a handful here had the patience and tenacity to get their hands on a fansub in the 90s.

[]: Re: Fan sub... B/ - Bl Post by Alaska Slim » Mon Aug 15, 2011 11:47 pm Bagheera wrote: No, I'm not. I'm saying your arguments are veering all over the place,

[]: Post by Tarnsman » Tue Aug 16, 2011 11:08 am Alaska Slim wrote: Even so, who made those tapes? ADV. Thus, you still owe them.

[]: Post by Alaska Slim » Tue Aug 16, 2011 1:22 pm Tarnsman wrote: Furthermore this notion that ADV was a godsend because everyone else at the time sucked is retarded. We should praise ADV for doing their job?

[]: Post by Bagheera » Tue Aug 16, 2011 1:38 pm Alaska Slim wrote: ADV can be tied directly with the high quality releases of Rebuild and other anime that we enjoy today. Simply because, with EVA, they raised the bar.

[]: Quality... B/ Post by Alaska Slim » Tue Aug 16, 2011 3:16 pm Bagheera wrote: Do you have evidence of this, or is it just rhetoric?

[]: Re: Quality... B/ Post by Bagheera » Tue Aug 16, 2011 4:01 pm Alaska Slim wrote: It's been cited as such , further, some state they did it again with Rahxephon .

[]: Post by Tarnsman » Tue Aug 16, 2011 4:21 pm Alaska Slim wrote: There's more to it than that, they treated Eva as if they cared for it. They're a "godsend" as you say, not just for doing "their job", but for doing more than they had to, and as a consequence, rising the benchmark in anime release quality, and what distributors were expected to do. ADV can be tied directly with the high quality releases of Rebuild and other anime that we enjoy today. Simply because, with EVA, they raised the bar.
